title: Runtime Configuration, Secrets, Environment Precedence, and Sensitive Data · Container Boundary Trace: Docker Credential Helper Rotation
summary: A service must receive database credentials and rotation-safe settings without baking them into images, logs, history, or source control. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker credential helper rotation increment in configuration and secret-flow contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use scoped tokens, credential stores or helpers, non-interactive CI login, logout, revocation, and rotation without command-line leakage.
Audit source, context, layers, history, cache, provenance, logs, inspect output, environment, crash reports, and registries for secret exposure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Credential Helper Rotation · Theory Docker Credential Helper Rotation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory build receives credentials through ARG and ENV; Compose interpolation prints them; image history and logs retain copies. Classify public config, build secrets, runtime secrets, redaction, and rotation.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Credential Helper Rotation contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Docker Credential Helper Rotation · Theory Docker Credential Helper Rotation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use scoped tokens, credential stores or helpers, non-interactive ci login, logout, revocation, and rotation without command-line leakage. For Predict Docker Credential Helper Rotation · Theory Docker Credential Helper Rotation, keep Docker Credential Helper Rotation connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example build receives credentials through ARG and ENV; Compose interpolation prints them; image history and logs retain copies. Classify public config, build secrets, runtime secrets, redaction, and rotation. Competency probe: Use scoped tokens, credential stores or helpers, non-interactive CI login, logout, revocation, and rotation without command-line leakage. A defensible solution applies Docker Credential Helper Rotation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to configuration and secret-flow contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-credential-helper-rotation — Use scoped tokens, credential stores or helpers, non-interactive CI login, logout, revocation, and rotation without command-line leakage.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-config-secrets-environment-2
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-config-secrets-environment-2
    artifact: configuration-boundary.yaml
    decision: worked docker config secrets environment 2
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: Docker Desktop VM with synchronized source files
    fault-injection: send termination during an in-flight durable operation
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      public-config: immutable-environment-map
      runtime-secret: mounted-file
      build-secret: buildkit-secret-mount
      logs: redact-values
      rotation: restart-with-versioned-reference
    verify: docker compose config --environment && docker image history --no-trunc candidate
    failure: Reject secrets in Dockerfile ENV, ARG, Compose interpolation output, image history, repository files, or diagnostic logs.
    repair: Separate public config from secrets, inject each at the narrowest lifetime, scan metadata, rotate the credential, and verify redaction.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: docker login credentials are always stored encrypted and cannot leak through process or CI output. This fails the Docker Credential Helper Rotation contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
